title: Surface area measures of log-concave functions
abstract: This paper's origins are in two papers: One by Colesanti and Fragalà studying the surface area measure of a log-concave function, and one by Cordero-Erausquin and Klartag regarding the moment measure of a convex function. These notions are the same, and in this paper we continue studying the same construction as well as its generalization.   In the first half the paper we prove a first variation formula for the integral of log-concave functions under minimal and optimal conditions. We also explain why this result is a common generalization of two known theorems from the above papers.   In the second half we extend the definition of the functional surface area measure to the L^p-setting, generalizing a classic definition of Lutwak. In this generalized setting we prove a functional Minkowski existence theorem for even measures. This is a partial extension of a theorem of Cordero-Erausquin and Klartag that handled the case p=1 for not necessarily even measures.
comment: Fixed LaTeX problem with non-English characters. Added Remark 3.3
